PAIN.quote: I was always playing military golf (left, right, left, right) and very frustrated
PAIN.context: Senior golfer describing slice frustration before finding equipment solution

PAIN.quote: With conventional drivers and fairway 3s, I was always playing military golf
PAIN.context: 60+ golfer with back surgeries describing driver struggles

PAIN.quote: Too hard to find the sweet spot. Teed to high or to low. Couldn't get lift out of the fairway
PAIN.context: 71-year-old golfer's first impression of infomercial driver

PAIN.quote: I have been trying to order the club and finally got through and was told there is a 10 week back log. Not happy
BUYER BEWARE: Revolution Golf and the (T)less driver are junk! DO NOT buy the RXS Teeless driver!!
A circular crack has appeared in the crown despite only playing 15 to 20 rounds since purchased
I purchased one directly from RevolutionGolf in Nov 2018. Over the next 6-months I played 20 rounds with my new Teeless driver. Yesterday I used it for the first three holes and the top cracked!!
PAIN.context: Quality failure and poor customer service

PAIN.quote: I wrote RG and they said it was outside of the return policy and therefore they would do nothing for me
This driver hit consistently 15-20 yards shorter than my regular driver
PAIN.context: 78-year-old experienced golfer disappointed with MaxVert distance

PAIN.quote: I absolutely hated the club from the first time I tried it... This driver hit weaker than any driver I've had in my bag
PAIN.context: 65+ years golf experience, 10 handicap, college golfer background

PAIN.quote: cost me $34 to send back the Maxvert
The GX7 team were great in the sales end, quick to take my money, but club was never received as promised. Not able to get an answer to emails for resolution
I purchased a tiny training aid and was apparently nefariously signed up for a $29 monthly subscription and have now paid $522 in monthly subscription fees
I have no clue what this monthly subscription even does, and I've never even logged in that I am aware of. What kind of business does this?
I made a one-time purchase of The Pendulum Swing Sequence and unknowingly was signed for a monthly membership
I purchased two golf clubs online. After 2 1/2 months and several broken promises the golf clubs never arrived
A couple of days after my refund they charge me another $300 for a membership that I never ask for
Phone calls become sales spiels (very hard pressed) for all kinds of things, with no opportunity to speak with a live person
This subscription was not discussed but was quietly tucked in with the purchase of the club
I do not get emailed a receipt for these monthly charges and did not notice them until I reviewed by credit card statement

HOPE.quote: I use the GX7 now as my driver and fairway wood. I leave my driver and 3 wood at home
With my GX7, I can go help others find their ball and help them catch up to me
My scores are upper 80s or low 90s but I smile and enjoy golf now
Everyone I know who has a GX7 too, don't want to part with it. My friends want to buy used ones from those who have them and they won't let them go
My friend is 67 and fits the description of someone who would benefit from having this club in his bag. His scoring is better this Spring, and a big reason is his success with this club

Since he's got this his fairway has improved so much, he didnt' even lose distance because he didn't use to hit the driver solid at now. Now he is consistently around 200yrds in the fairway. He gets 80% fairways easy
It makes a huge difference, if you are willing to use it as directed
HOPE.context: MaxVert defender - works if used correctly

HOPE.quote: you will be rewarded with consistently straight shots with less of the spin that takes a ball off target, especially slices
I cranked out multiple 280-yard-plus drives with the SF1, a good poke regardless of the driver in hand by my standards
On average, the SF1 was only eight yards behind my gamer... that's a huge win
If you can be 30 or so yards closer to the center and only a few yards shorter, you could save a lot of strokes
HOPE.context: Accuracy vs distance tradeoff value proposition

HOPE.quote: I striped it right down the middle. Same story on the next hole, and the next
this SF1 driver has been a lifesaver when my slice decides to show up out of nowhere
hit a few drives 20-30 yards further than before
I'm hanging out with the big hitters on most of my drives now
I hit this club almost as long off the ground as I do the tee... This club is for real

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: It is a THREE WOOD. Yes, it's perimeter weighted and has a playable sole, but it won't go as far as your driver unless you can't hit your driver
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Honest assessment of infomercial driver category

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: In all their promotions the two bits of information they leave out are the club length and lie angle
I asked how is that like a 7 iron? He said because you use a descending blow like an iron. Liars and deceivers!
at 43" and 14* I would just go get a mini driver from Callaway or Taylormade for probably $100
My impression on it is it's a slickly marketed mini driver, and I personally think you'd be better off getting a used Bertha Mini, Aeroburner Mini
I tend to stay away from some of these brands because they are just unproven and typically use cheap materials
last time I checked every manufacturer offers a higher lofted, forgiving, draw biased driver

it is still possible to hit a slice with the SF1 driver. If you visit the product page and believe every word there, you might be tempted to think you've hit your last slice ever, but don't get too excited
Some of the claims and promises that come with the SF1 package are hokey
Touring professionals use less forgiving drivers. They prefer less forgiving drivers... The casual weekend golfer tends to have a simpler, more direct goal

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I have tried many golf clubs that claim to be longer against my current club and only one of the 8 different drivers really was longer
This web sight reads like a sales pitch. The only real review is from real amateur golfers who try the club for several rounds
I brought a T Less Driver and have played with it for about 25 to 30 rounds and it worked quite well, but now it has a crack on the top of the head
I have also been sizing my drivers at a more normal 44.5" playing length for several years now. And I agree that shortening the driver shaft length makes it much easier to square the face
I have a GX7 which is essentially the same concept. T Less was not as long as GX7

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Square Face Technology helps golfers get their face pointing at the target instead of being open
AeroSquare Crown increases toe rotation at impact so golfers can more easily square the clubhead
Anti-Slice Face Curvature produces draw spin to promote a straighter ball flight
Feedback Takeaway Tracks that promote a takeaway that's square to the target
two Counter-Slice weights strategically engineered and placed to further promote a square clubface
The face angle itself is offset and calibrated for a draw
Shaft Shift Technology, nudging the shaft a little closer to the center of the clubhead and more in line with the center of gravity
Perimeter Payload - 25 grams of weight to the heel... lightens the load in the toe, which makes it easier to release the club at impact
More upright lie that helps you find more length in your swing with a vertical motion
Fairway Finder Alignment Guide: three crisp lines on the crown of the driver to help you square the clubface
Ground Control Sole designed to glide easily through any turf
center of gravity is set farther back, toward the rear of the clubhead. This rearward COG accounts for the Spectrum CT clubface
13-degree loft... The adjusted center of gravity also helps you get that high launch angle

VILLAIN.quote: 786 total complaints in the last 3 years. 221 complaints closed in the last 12 months
The offer stated that once the trial period ended, the membership would automatically continue at $29 per month unless canceled
They have access to my credit card and continue to charge me willy nilly
This is a poorly made product and a gimmick
Neither Revolution Golf nor the manufacturer were standing by their product
I have a friend who's a sucker for those kind of clubs, they rarely stay in his bag for very long
another 'as seen on TV' gimmick club like the Alien wedge, Hammer driver, or S7K stand-alone putter
What if we have tried it and still think its poorly made and doesn't work very well?
the heads are made out of a lightweight steel
According to their own Customer Service department, they have had numerous complaints about faulty Teeless drivers
